summary: Added a new C translation unit for Peach float behavior and removed the corresponding early `ftPe_Float` routines from `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s`, while keeping some routines behind `MUST_MATCH` inline-asm fallbacks. The PR also made supporting fighter/input/Peach-attribute naming improvements across many fighter files so the new C could express float state and input tests more clearly.
what_changed: Created `src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_Float.c` with C implementations or partial implementations for Peach float entry, animation, IASA, physics, collision, and float-fall direction logic.

Deleted the matching `ftPe_Float` block from `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s`, leaving later Peach init/float-fall assembly and converting remaining sdata2 literals from raw `.4byte` to typed `.float`/`.double` notation.
Added `$(BUILD_DIR)/src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_Float.c.o` to `obj_files.mk` before the remaining Peach assembly object.

Renamed Peach fighter vars in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/types.h`, notably `x222C` to `has_float`, `x2230` to `x4`, and Peach DAT attrs to `ftPe_DatAttrs` with names such as `floatfallf_anim_start`, `floatfallb_anim_start`, and `floatfall_anim_start_offset`.
Updated Peach init to initialize the renamed Peach float state and use `ftPe_DatAttrs` in load/copy attribute macros.
Replaced `HSD_Buttons` enum constants with `typedef u32 HSD_Pad` plus `static HSD_Pad const` bit constants such as `HSD_Pad_B`, `HSD_Pad_DpadDown`, and new combined `HSD_Pad_XY`.
Updated many fighter files from old fighter field names such as `fp->x4_fighterKind` and `fp->x2219_flag.bits.b0` to shorter names such as `fp->kind` and `fp->x2219_b0`.
Adjusted `efAsync_Spawn` declaration to include `HSD_JObj*` for its final fixed argument and include `baselib/forward.h` instead of the full gobj header.
smart_moves: Separated the new C object from the residual Peach assembly in `obj_files.mk`, allowing only the translated float routines to move out of `ftPe_Init.s` without forcing the rest of the file to match at once.

Used helper predicates such as `checkStartFloatInput`, `checkContinueFloatInput`, `getFloatDir`, and `updateFloatDir` to expose the control-flow meaning of input and direction tests while still preserving the original function boundaries.

Kept difficult routines guarded with `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` inline assembly, making the file usable for matching builds while documenting the intended C equivalent under the non-asm branch.
Introduced `HSD_Pad_XY` so Peach float input can be expressed as `heldInputs & HSD_Pad_XY` rather than repeating or relying on anonymous bit masks.
Renamed Peach-specific state and DAT attributes before using them heavily in `ftPe_Float.c`, reducing unknown-offset noise in the new decompilation.
decomp_lessons: Partial file migration is viable: add a new C object immediately before the remaining asm object and delete only the matched assembly range, preserving link/order constraints.

When matching fighter action code, struct field naming often has to be improved project-wide first; here, `fp->kind`, `fp->x2219_b0`, `HSD_Pad_*`, and Peach float fields made the C code simpler and more reusable.
For motion-state transitions, recover semantic constants early: this PR names Peach float/fall motion states and DAT animation-start fields rather than leaving all values as offsets.
Inline `MUST_MATCH` assembly can be used as a bridge for routines that are understood but not yet cleanly matched in C, especially when a PR goal is to partially match a whole action group.
Effect-spawn prototypes matter for matching and readability; refining `efAsync_Spawn` to take an `HSD_JObj*` made the call sites in fighter code more accurately typed.
naming_conventions: `HSD_BUTTON_*` enum names were replaced by `HSD_Pad_*` static constants, using Pascal-ish suffixes such as `DpadDown`, `Start`, `LR`, `AB`, and `XY`.
The fighter kind field convention moved from `x4_fighterKind` to `kind`.
The common fighter bitfield access convention moved from `x2219_flag.bits.b0` to direct `x2219_b0`.
Peach DAT attributes use the `ftPe_DatAttrs` type name and descriptive float-fall fields such as `floatfallf_anim_start`, `floatfallb_anim_start`, and `floatfall_anim_start_offset`.
Peach fighter vars retained some unknown suffix names (`x4`, `x8`, `xC`, etc.) but named the key boolean at offset `fp+222C` as `has_float`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved a contiguous assembly range for `ftPe_8011BA54` through `ftPe_8011BD6C` out of `ftPe_Init.s` into `ftPe_Float.c` while retaining later Peach assembly in the same original asm file.
Used `float const ftPe_Float_804D97C0 = 0` and `ftPe_Float_804D97C4 = 1` for sdata2 constants needed by inline asm and C.
Used `#pragma push`, inline `asm`, `nofralloc`, and original instruction comments under `MUST_MATCH` for functions that were not cleanly matched in portable C.
Provided non-`MUST_MATCH` C alternatives under `#else`, which documents intended behavior even when the matching path still relies on assembly.
Converted remaining sdata2 entries in the asm file from raw hexadecimal `.4byte` values to readable `.float` and `.double` directives.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body evidence is minimal: it contains the title and an embedded image, but no detailed written rationale.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match the `MUST_MATCH` inline asm routines in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_Float.c`.
Search for follow-up renames of Peach fighter vars `fv.pe.x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x10`, `x14`, `x18`, and `x20`.
Search for project-wide adoption or later revision of the `HSD_Pad_*` input constants introduced here.
Inspect later changes to `ftPe_DatAttrs` for more complete naming of the `xC` and `x10` fields.
